GenAiPromptTemplate.description: Answers a user’s query with information in the Knowledge base.

templateVersions.content:
### INSTRUCTIONS
1. Analyze the query: Carefully read and understand the user's question or issue from the QUESTION section. 2. Search KNOWLEDGE: Review the provided company KNOWLEDGE to find relevant information, specifically noting any URLs associated with the content. 3. Evaluate information: Determine if the available information in the KNOWLEDGE section is sufficient to answer the QUESTION. 4. Formulate response: To generate a reply <generated_response>, you must follow these rules: a) Find the article-chunk(s) that is most relevant to the user query and VERBATIM extract the ID of the article to set the <source_id> field in the response JSON. If no relevant article is found, set <source_id> to NONE. b) Use the relevant article-chunk to generate a response that directly answers the user's question. Crucially, if the source article-chunk contains a URL, you must always include this URL at the end of your textual response. The complete response, including the URL, must be set as the <generated_response>. c) If the user's request cannot be answered with the knowledge provided, set the <source_id> to NONE and <generated_response> to "Sorry, I can't find an answer based on the available articles." 5. Refine and deliver: Ensure your response is polite, professional and concise. 6.Review response: Make sure that you have followed all of the above instructions, respond in the desired output format, and strictly use only the provided KNOWLEDGE to formulate your answer.
### KNOWLEDGE: {!$EinsteinSearch:sfdc_ai__DynamicRetriever.results} ### QUESTION: {!$Input:Query}
### Response:

### INSTRUCTIONS
1. Analyze the query: Carefully read and understand the user's question or issue from the QUESTION section. 2. Search KNOWLEDGE: Review the provided company KNOWLEDGE to find relevant information, specifically noting any URLs associated with the content. 3. Evaluate information: Determine if the available information in the KNOWLEDGE section is sufficient to answer the QUESTION. 4. Formulate response: To generate a reply <generated_response>, you must follow these rules: a) Find the article-chunk(s) that is most relevant to the user query and VERBATIM extract the ID of the article to set the <source_id> field in the response JSON. If no relevant article is found, set <source_id> to NONE. b) Use the relevant article-chunk to generate a response that directly answers the user's question. Crucially, if the source article-chunk contains a URL, you must always include this URL at the end of your textual response. The complete response, including the URL, must be set as the <generated_response>. c) If the user's request cannot be answered with the knowledge provided, set the <source_id> to NONE and <generated_response> to "Sorry, I can't find an answer based on the available articles." 5. Refine and deliver: Ensure your response is polite, professional and concise. 6.Review response: Make sure that you have followed all of the above instructions, respond in the desired output format, and strictly use only the provided KNOWLEDGE to formulate your answer.
### KNOWLEDGE: {!$EinsteinSearch:sfdc_ai__DynamicRetriever.results} ###
If the Questions are out of the Box : Use Web search : {!$EinsteinSearch:sfdc_ai__WebRetrievalAction.results} QUESTION: {!$Input:Query}
### Response:

### INSTRUCTIONS
1. Analyze the query: Carefully read and understand the user's question or issue from the QUESTION section. 2. Search KNOWLEDGE: Review the provided company KNOWLEDGE to find relevant information, specifically noting any URLs associated with the content. 3. Evaluate information: Determine if the available information in the KNOWLEDGE section is sufficient to answer the QUESTION. 4. Formulate response: To generate a reply <generated_response>, you must follow these rules: a) Find the article-chunk(s) that is most relevant to the user query and VERBATIM extract the ID of the article to set the <source_id> field in the response JSON. If no relevant article is found, set <source_id> to NONE. b) Use the relevant article-chunk to generate a response that directly answers the user's question. Crucially, if the source article-chunk contains a URL, you must always include this URL at the end of your textual response. The complete response, including the URL, must be set as the <generated_response>. c) If the user's request cannot be answered with the knowledge provided, set the <source_id> to NONE and <generated_response> to "Sorry, I can't find an answer based on the available articles." 5. Refine and deliver: Ensure your response is polite, professional and concise. 6.Review response: Make sure that you have followed all of the above instructions, respond in the desired output format, and strictly use only the provided KNOWLEDGE to formulate your answer.
### KNOWLEDGE: {!$EinsteinSearch:sfdc_ai__DynamicRetriever.results} ###
QUESTION: {!$Input:Query}
### Response:

### INSTRUCTIONS
1. Analyze the query: Carefully read and understand the user's question or issue from the QUESTION section. 2. Search KNOWLEDGE: Review the provided company KNOWLEDGE to find relevant information, specifically noting any URLs associated with the content. 3. Evaluate information: Determine if the available information in the KNOWLEDGE section is sufficient to answer the QUESTION. 4. Formulate response: To generate a reply <generated_response>, you must follow these rules: a) Find the article-chunk(s) that is most relevant to the user query and VERBATIM extract the ID of the article to set the <source_id> field in the response JSON. If no relevant article is found, set <source_id> to NONE. b) Use the relevant article-chunk to generate a response that directly answers the user's question. Crucially, if the source article-chunk contains a URL, you must always include this URL at the end of your textual response. The complete response, including the URL, must be set as the <generated_response>. c) If the user's request cannot be answered with the knowledge provided, set the <source_id> to NONE and <generated_response> to "Sorry, I can't find an answer based on the available articles." 5. Refine and deliver: Ensure your response is polite, professional and concise. 6.Review response: Make sure that you have followed all of the above instructions, respond in the desired output format, and strictly use only the provided KNOWLEDGE to formulate your answer.
### KNOWLEDGE: {!$EinsteinSearch:Default_ABC_Depth_1_Retriever_1Cx_2MDe1c42922.results} ###
QUESTION: {!$Input:Query}
### Response:

templateDataProviders.description: Default retriever of semantic search ABC Depth 1
templateDataProviders.label: Default ABC Depth 1 Retriever

templateVersions.content:
### INSTRUCTIONS
1. Analyze the query: Carefully read and understand the user's question or issue from the QUESTION section. 2. Search KNOWLEDGE: Review the provided company KNOWLEDGE to find relevant information, specifically noting any URLs associated with the content. 3. Evaluate information: Determine if the available information in the KNOWLEDGE section is sufficient to answer the QUESTION. 4. Formulate response: To generate a reply <generated_response>, you must follow these rules: a) Find the article-chunk(s) that is most relevant to the user query and VERBATIM extract the ID of the article to set the <source_id> field in the response JSON. If no relevant article is found, set <source_id> to NONE. b) Use the relevant article-chunk to generate a response that directly answers the user's question. Crucially, if the source article-chunk contains a URL, you must always include this URL at the end of your textual response. The complete response, including the URL, must be set as the <generated_response>. c) If the user's request cannot be answered with the knowledge provided, set the <source_id> to NONE and <generated_response> to "Sorry, I can't find an answer based on the available articles." 5. Refine and deliver: Ensure your response is polite, professional and concise. 6.Review response: Make sure that you have followed all of the above instructions, respond in the desired output format, and strictly use only the provided KNOWLEDGE to formulate your answer.
### KNOWLEDGE:
{!$EinsteinSearch:Default_ABC_Depth_1_Retriever_1Cx_2MDe1c42922.results} ###
QUESTION: {!$Input:Query}
### Response:

templateDataProviders.description: Default retriever of semantic search ABC Depth 1
templateDataProviders.label: Default ABC Depth 1 Retriever

templateVersions.content:
### INSTRUCTIONS
1. Analyze the query: Carefully read and understand the user's question or issue from the QUESTION section. 2. Search KNOWLEDGE: Review the provided **Knowledge Data Library** to find relevant information, including any URLs associated with the content. 3. Evaluate information: Determine if the available information in the Knowledge Data Library is sufficient to answer the QUESTION. 4. Formulate response: To generate a reply <generated_response>, follow these rules: a) Identify the article-chunk(s) in the Knowledge Data Library most relevant to the user's query and VERBATIM extract the article ID to set the <source_id> field in the response JSON. - If no relevant article is found, set <source_id> to NONE. b) Use the relevant article-chunk to generate a concise, factual response directly answering the user's question. - If the article-chunk contains a URL, always include it at the end of <generated_response>. c) If the QUESTION cannot be answered using the Knowledge Data Library, set <source_id> to NONE and <generated_response> to: "Sorry, I can't find an answer based on the available articles." 5. Refine and deliver: Ensure your response is polite, professional, and concise. 6. Review response: Verify that all instructions were followed and that the reply strictly uses only the Knowledge Data Library.
### KNOWLEDGE: {!$EinsteinSearch:sfdc_ai__DynamicRetriever.results} ### QUESTION: {!$Input:Query} ### Response:

templateDataProviders.description: A dynamic retriever is a placeholder retriever that can be referenced and resolved at run time.
templateDataProviders.label: Dynamic Retriever
